channel.title: Veille métier du 2026-04-10
channel.description: Veille quotidienne assurance, finance, banque et régulation.

item.title: The EBA consults on revised Guidelines on limits on exposures to shadow banking entities under the Capital Requirements Regulation
item.description: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today launched a public consultation on revised Guidelines on limits on exposures to shadow banking entities carrying out banking activities outside a regulated framework. The revised Guidelines aim to align with the updated EU large-exposure reporting framework and to support sound risk management and governance practices across institutions. — Thématique réglementation ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.

item.title: EIOPA statement for ECON public hearing on the role of pension funds and insurers in capital markets on 8 April 2026
item.description: EIOPA statement for ECON public hearing on the role of pension funds and insurers in capital markets on 8 April 2026 — Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (eiopa media) ; impact marché.

item.title: The EBA publishes list of known data point model issues to enhance transparency and support reporting institutions
item.description: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today announced that it will begin publishing, on a regular basis, a list of known issues related to the data point model (DPM) framework. The list will include information on available workarounds and indicative timelines for resolution. This initiative is intended to improve transparency and to support reporting institutions by providing a single reference point for recurring technical questions. — Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; information très récente.

item.title: EIOPA and ESM staff propose mechanism to better manage fallout from outsized natural catastrophes
item.description: EIOPA and the ESM published a discussion paper on natural catastrophe risk management. Amid mounting losses from extreme weather events across Europe, the paper quantifies the benefits of a European risk-sharing mechanism—composed of a natural catastrophe insurance pool and a loan-based backstop. — Thématique assurance ; source jugée fiable (eiopa media) ; impact assurance.
